title: 'Balance and stability in the Arabian Gulf are now just memories'
subhed: On February 28, a new chapter began for the Gulf petro-monarchies, now mourning the loss of a bygone regional order. The attack ordered by Donald Trump left them exposed to retaliation from the Iranian regime, observes columnist Gilles Paris.

title: The deadly cheetah trafficking between Somaliland and the Gulf
subhed: The feline is highly sought after by wealthy collectors of wild animals, who are numerous on the Arabian Peninsula. Across the way, in the Horn of Africa, Somaliland has become the main hub of a thriving trafficking network, even as the species itself is under threat.

title: On housing, Mayor Grégoire wants people to 'still dream of living' in Paris
subhed: The new mayor has pledged to create 30,000 new social housing units and 30,000 additional 'affordable' homes – an ambitious goal. To reach it, Paris will have to nearly double its annual production of public housing.
